description: ML model predicting disease outbreak probability by Local Government Area using historical case and environmental data
primary_purpose: Public health outbreak prediction and early warning
data_inputs: Patient case reports; hospital admission data; climate data; population density data; LGA-level reporting
outputs: Risk probability score (Low/Medium/High/Critical) per LGA; alert notifications to NCDC epidemiologists
vendor_or_internal: External - Africa CDC / WHO AFRO technical support
affected_persons: General population - all 36 states and FCT
notes: System used in critical public health infrastructure. Outputs inform outbreak alerts and emergency resource deployment. Data quality gaps in rural LGA reporting identified. Model explainability limited. Quarterly review recommended but not consistently implemented.

description: Computer vision model detecting anomalies in chest X-rays and CT scans; flags suspected TB, pneumonia, and cancer cases for radiologist review
business_unit: Radiology Department - Lagos University Teaching Hospital (LUTH)
data_inputs: Anonymised patient chest X-rays and CT scan images
outputs: Anomaly probability score per image; recommended review flag (Urgent/Routine/Clear)
notes: EU AI Act Annex III - AI used in medical device/diagnostic context. No formal conformity assessment conducted. Training data sourced primarily from non-Nigerian patient cohorts. No audit trail for AI recommendations. Mandatory radiologist review in place but not formally documented.

description: Predictive model stratifying maternal mortality risk at antenatal registration and recommending specialist referral for high-risk pregnancies
business_unit: National Primary Health Care Development Agency (NPHCDA)
primary_purpose: Maternal health risk stratification and referral
data_inputs: Age; parity; blood pressure; haemoglobin levels; gestation week; LGA location; facility type
outputs: Risk category (Low/Medium/High); referral recommendation to community health worker
affected_persons: Pregnant women at antenatal registration - 12 states active
notes: EU AI Act Annex III - AI system in healthcare affecting vulnerable population. Model trained on urban facility data only; rural performance not validated. CHEWs lack training to interpret AI outputs. No feedback mechanism to retrain model on updated Nigerian data. 24-month review cycle inadequate.

description: Health data aggregation and ML forecasting platform generating trend analysis and resource planning recommendations
business_unit: National Health Management Information System (NHMIS) - FMoH
primary_purpose: National health data analytics and resource planning support
data_inputs: Facility-level health service data; drug supply chain records; staffing data; disease burden statistics
outputs: Trend reports; demand forecasts; resource allocation recommendations (non-binding)
vendor_or_internal: External - University of Oslo (DHIS2); FMoH customisation team
affected_persons: Internal health planners and Ministry of Health officials
notes: Human decision-maker retains full control - AI output is advisory only. Data completeness gaps from low-reporting facilities identified. ML forecasting module added 2023 without formal governance review. Transparency of forecast methodology should be documented.

description: Conversational AI system providing autonomous symptom assessment and triage guidance to patients via SMS and WhatsApp
primary_purpose: Patient-facing autonomous triage and health guidance
data_inputs: Patient-reported symptoms (text input); self-reported age; LGA location
outputs: Triage recommendation (Self-care/Primary Care/Emergency); facility referral suggestion
notes: CRITICAL GOVERNANCE GAP. Highest-risk system in inventory. Autonomous AI with no mandatory human review at point of decision. No accountable owner assigned. No adverse event reporting mechanism. Incorrect triage could delay emergency care. Immediate governance review recommended.

description: Machine learning model identifying likely contacts and transmission chains during disease outbreaks using call data records
business_unit: Nigeria Centre for Disease Control (NCDC) - Surveillance Division
data_inputs: Anonymised call data records (CDR); case interview data; geographic location data
outputs: Ranked list of priority contacts for field follow-up; transmission chain visualisation
vendor_or_internal: External - Africa CDC emergency support tool
affected_persons: Individuals identified as potential disease contacts
notes: Activated during COVID-19 response. Use of call data records raises privacy concerns - data minimisation and consent protocols not publicly documented. Governance framework should be established prior to next activation. Data retention policy for CDR data not confirmed.
